content:
The message may have been all about the changes occuring with Yahoo. So lets deal with Yahoo first. Recent updated info

  • https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/thunderbird-and-yahoo

I would suggest you completely remove any stored passwords for the 'Yahoo' accounts first so there will be no confusion.

  • Settings > Privacy & Security
  • Scroll down to Passwords
  • Click on 'Saved Passwords'
  • Click on 'show Passwords'
  • then Remove any line that mentions the 'Username' = Yahoo email account. You mau only have one that begins with 'oauth://...' but if you see others that start with imap://..or smtp:// for the same yahoo account then make sure they are all removed.
  • Click on 'Close'
  • Now Exit Thunderbird, wait a few moments for background processes to compete and the restart Thunderbird.

You should get a prompt from Yahoo to enter password

IF you have more than one Yahoo account or additional accounts that uses the yahoo server such as AOL. Eg: you have one yahoo and one aol OR two or more yahoo accounts then you need to alter the following preference first.

  • Settings > General
  • Scroll to bottom and click on 'Config Editor'
  • It opens in a new tab
  • In search type: oauth.use
  • look for : mailnews.oauth.usePrivateBrowse
  • toggle from 'false' to 'TRUE'
  • Exit Thunderbird, wait a few moments for background processes to compete and the restart Thunderbird.
creator: Toad-Hall

content:
What is your computer operating system? I believe this is a system problem, not a Thunderbird problem.

In Windows, it sometimes happens that a window winds up off-screen, that is, it's there, but there's little or none of it available to grab with the mouse. The fix in this case, is to use Alt+Tab to bring the errant window into the foreground (even if it's not visible.) Then press Alt+Spacebar, which will open up a context menu, one of the options will be Move. You can activate that option with the keystrokes Alt+Spacebar, then "M". You should then see a 4-sided arrow which will let you use the arrow keys or mouse to maneuver the window back to a better position.

content:
Se a volte funziona ed altre no, il problema lo dovresti segnalare a TIM.

PIÙ in generale comunque, Per poter inquadrare il problema prova a fornire queste informazioni:

  1. Versione di Thunderbird
  2. Versione del sistema operativo
  3. Provider delle caselle email (Gmail, Libero, Hotmail, etc.)
  4. Tipologia di account (POP3, IMAP)
  5. Estensioni installate
  6. Messaggi di errore che vengono visualizzati durante l'utilizzo di Thunderbird
  7. Software di sicurezza eventualmente presenti (Antivirus e simili)
  8. Screenshot del problema (se utile a chiarire il contesto)
